chapterTitle: How to Talk About Personality Types

mainWords.meaning: I or self

derivedWords.definition: a self-centered person who believes in self-interest
derivedWords.example: An egoist always asks 'What's in it for me?' before acting.

derivedWords.definition: an arrogant talker who brags about accomplishments
derivedWords.example: The egotist spent the entire dinner talking about his promotions.
He is so egocentric that he thinks every conversation is about him.
Her extreme vanity made her behave like an egomaniac.

mainWords.meaning: other

derivedWords.definition: a person unselfishly concerned for the welfare of others
derivedWords.example: The altruist spent her weekends volunteering at the shelter.

derivedWords.definition: take turns or skip one and take the other
derivedWords.example: We will alternate driving shifts to avoid getting tired.
She made some alterations to the dress so it fit perfectly.
The minor accident led to an altercation between the drivers.
Bruce Wayne's alterego is Batman.

mainWords.meaning: turn

derivedWords.definition: a shy, reticent person focused on their own thoughts
derivedWords.example: As an introvert, she preferred reading a book over going to a loud party.
The extrovert made friends with everyone in the room within minutes.

derivedWords.definition: a person whose personality has features of both
derivedWords.example: He is an ambivert who enjoys socializing but also needs quiet time.

derivedWords.definition: to turn upside down or inside out
derivedWords.example: Invert the jar to let the liquid drain completely.

derivedWords.definition: to return to a previous state or habit
derivedWords.example: After a week of clean eating, he reverted to his junk food habits.

mainWords.meaning: hate

derivedWords.definition: a person who dislikes humankind and avoids society
derivedWords.example: The hermit was accused of being a misanthrope who hated visitors.

derivedWords.definition: a person who dislikes or is prejudiced against women
derivedWords.example: His offensive remarks revealed him to be a misogynist.
The lifelong bachelor was a proud misogamist.

derivedWords.definition: showing hatred or distrust of humankind
derivedWords.example: He held a misanthropic view of world events.

derivedWords.definition: hatred of or prejudice against women
derivedWords.example: The committee worked to eliminate misogyny in the workplace.

chapterTitle: How to Talk About Doctors & Specialists

mainWords.meaning: skin

derivedWords.definition: a doctor who specializes in skin diseases
derivedWords.example: The dermatologist prescribed a cream for his eczema.
Touching the poison ivy caused severe dermatitis.
The sun burned the epidermis of his shoulders.

derivedWords.definition: one who stuffs and mounts skins of dead animals
derivedWords.example: The hunter took the deer head to a taxidermist.
Elephants and rhinos are classified as pachyderms.

mainWords.meaning: mind or soul

derivedWords.definition: a medical practitioner specializing in mental illness
derivedWords.example: The psychiatrist diagnosed the patient's chemical imbalance.

derivedWords.definition: a specialist in the study of the mind and behavior
derivedWords.example: The psychologist conducted research on cognitive development.

derivedWords.definition: suffering from a chronic mental disorder with abnormal behavior
derivedWords.example: The thriller featured a psychopathic villain.

derivedWords.definition: physical illness caused or aggravated by mental factors
derivedWords.example: His chronic stomach aches were largely psychosomatic.

derivedWords.definition: the human soul, mind, or spirit
derivedWords.example: Exploring the depths of the human psyche is fascinating.

mainWords.meaning: eye

derivedWords.definition: a doctor who specializes in eye anatomy and diseases
derivedWords.example: She visited the ophthalmologist for regular eye pressure tests.

derivedWords.definition: the branch of medicine concerned with study and treatment of eye disorders
derivedWords.example: He chose to specialize in ophthalmology after medical school.

derivedWords.definition: relating to the eye and its diseases
derivedWords.example: The pharmacist recommended ophthalmic drops for dryness.

derivedWords.definition: an instrument for inspecting the retina and parts of the eye
derivedWords.example: The doctor used an ophthalmoscope to peer into the pupil.

mainWords.meaning: straight or correct

derivedWords.definition: a dentist who specializes in straightening teeth
derivedWords.example: The orthodontist tightened her braces.

derivedWords.definition: a specialist in correcting deformities of bones or muscles
derivedWords.example: The orthopedist cast his broken leg.

derivedWords.definition: conforming to what is traditionally accepted as right
derivedWords.example: His orthodox methods produced reliable, if boring, results.

derivedWords.definition: the conventional spelling system of a language
derivedWords.example: English orthography is notoriously difficult due to silent letters.

mainWords.meaning: 欢快友善的 / friendly and lively

derivedWords.definition: adj. 活泼的；快活的；有生气的
derivedWords.example: She was a vivacious hostess who kept the conversation lively all evening.

derivedWords.definition: adj. 生动的；鲜明的；鲜艳的
derivedWords.example: He gave a vivid description of the sunset over the mountains.

derivedWords.definition: vi. 复兴；复活；苏醒；恢复精神
derivedWords.example: A cup of hot tea revived her spirits after the long journey.
The ethics of vivisection have been debated in scientific communities.
Most mammals are viviparous creatures.
Birds and reptiles are oviparous species.

derivedWords.definition: n. [植] 子房；[解剖] 卵巢
derivedWords.example: The ovaries release eggs periodically in female organisms.

derivedWords.definition: adj. [解剖] 卵巢的；子房的
derivedWords.example: Ovarian health is essential for reproduction.

derivedWords.definition: n. [细胞][组织] 卵；卵子；卵形装饰，复数ova
derivedWords.example: The ovum is fertilized by the sperm cell.

derivedWords.definition: adj. 至关重要的；生死攸关的；有活力的
derivedWords.example: Water is vital for the survival of all living things.

derivedWords.definition: vt. 使…复活；使…复兴；使…恢复生气
derivedWords.example: The new park helped revitalize the neighborhood.
A long illness can devitalize even the strongest athletes.

derivedWords.definition: n. [生化] 维生素；[生化] 维他命
derivedWords.example: Fresh fruits are packed with essential vitamins.
She had a remarkable joie de vivre that inspired everyone around her.

derivedWords.definition: （法）讲究美食及生活享受的人
derivedWords.example: He lived as a bon vivant, dining in top restaurants every week.

mainWords.meaning: 不知疲倦的 / tireless

derivedWords.definition: n. 疲劳，疲乏；杂役，adj. 疲劳的，vt. 使疲劳；使心智衰弱
derivedWords.example: Long hours of screen time can cause muscle fatigue.

derivedWords.definition: adj. 天真的；坦白的；正直的；朴实的
derivedWords.example: Her ingenuous smile disarmed her skeptics.

derivedWords.definition: adj. 有独创性的；机灵的，精制的；心灵手巧的
derivedWords.example: The mechanic came up with an ingenious solution to the engine problem.

derivedWords.definition: adj. 虚伪的；不诚实的；不老实的；狡猾的
derivedWords.example: It was disingenuous of him to pretend he didn't know about the rules.

derivedWords.definition: adj. 轻信的；因轻信而产生的
derivedWords.example: A credulous customer will believe any advertisement claim.

derivedWords.definition: n. 信用，信誉；[金融] 贷款；学分；信任；声望
derivedWords.example: The store offers interest-free credit to new customers.
We need a credible witness to verify the account.

derivedWords.definition: adj. 难以置信的，惊人的
derivedWords.example: The mountain view was absolutely incredible.
His personal credo was to treat everyone with dignity.
The company recruits employees regardless of race or creed.
You shouldn't give any credence to internet gossip.
She has all the necessary credentials to lead the research.
Scammers target gullible internet users.

mainWords.meaning: 有洞察力的，聪颖的 / keen-minded, wise

derivedWords.definition: adj. 明了的；清晰明白的；易懂的
derivedWords.example: She wrote a perspicuous and easily digestible policy summary.

derivedWords.definition: n. 精彩的表演；奇特的现象；出人意外的情况
derivedWords.example: The laser show was an impressive spectacle.

derivedWords.definition: adj. 壮观的，惊人的；公开展示的
derivedWords.example: The gymnast scored a spectacular victory.
The stadium was packed with excited spectators.
The health department inspected the kitchen conditions.

derivedWords.definition: n. 回顾，追溯 vi. 回顾，追溯；回想
derivedWords.example: In retrospect, we should have planned the route beforehand.

derivedWords.definition: n. 前途；预期；景色 vi. 勘探，找矿
derivedWords.example: The career prospects in AI look very promising.
Quiet evenings are a good time to introspect.

derivedWords.definition: adj. 细心的，周到的；慎重的
derivedWords.example: Officials were circumspect about revealing sensitive deal details.

mainWords.meaning: 心胸宽广的 / generous and forgiving

derivedWords.definition: adj. 宽宏大量的；有雅量的；宽大的
derivedWords.example: He was magnanimous in defeat, praising his opponent's play.

derivedWords.definition: adj. 全体一致的；意见一致的；无异议的
derivedWords.example: The board reached a unanimous decision on the merger.
She maintained her equanimity even during the crisis.

derivedWords.definition: n. 敌意；意图；基本态度；女性的男性意向
derivedWords.example: There was no personal animus between the rival executives.
We hold no animosity toward our competitors.

mainWords.meaning: 多才多艺的 / adaptable, skilled in many things

derivedWords.definition: adj. 多才多艺的；多功能的
derivedWords.example: He is a versatile actor who can play both comedy and tragedy.

mainWords.meaning: 坚韧的 / calm and unflinching in pain or trouble

derivedWords.example: Her stoical response to the bad news impressed her coworkers.

mainWords.meaning: 勇敢的 / fearless, brave

derivedWords.example: The intrepid explorer set out into the uncharted desert.

mainWords.meaning: 才华横溢 / sparkling, brilliantly clever

derivedWords.definition: adj. 闪烁的；才华横溢的
derivedWords.example: The conversation was scintillating and full of wit.

mainWords.meaning: 彬彬有礼的 / sophisticated, refined

derivedWords.definition: adj. 彬彬有礼的；都市风光的
derivedWords.example: He was a smooth, urbane gentleman.

derivedWords.definition: adj. 郊区的，城郊的；土气的；见闻不广的
derivedWords.example: They moved from the city center to a quiet suburban street.
The interurban railway connected the two neighboring cities.
The intraurban transit system was highly efficient.

derivedWords.definition: n. 远郊地区；城市远郊
derivedWords.example: Many commuters live in the exurbs and travel into the city daily.

mainWords.meaning: 严格执行纪律的人 / strict disciplinarian

derivedWords.definition: n. 严格执行纪律的人；要求极严的人
derivedWords.example: The military drill sergeant was a strict martinet.

mainWords.meaning: 谄媚者，奉承者 / brown-noser, flatterer

derivedWords.example: He is a sycophant who always agrees with the boss.

derivedWords.definition: n. 谄媚；奉承；追随；拍马屁
derivedWords.example: The leader was surrounded by constant sycophancy.

mainWords.meaning: 业余爱好者，半吊子 / amateur, dabbler

derivedWords.definition: n. 业余爱好者；半吊子
derivedWords.example: She is a dilettante who paints only for leisure.

derivedWords.definition: n. 业余的艺术爱好；浅薄涉猎
derivedWords.example: His dilettantism kept him from ever mastering any single instrument.

mainWords.meaning: 泼妇 / loud-mouthed woman

derivedWords.example: The virago screamed at the street vendor.
He spent his entire patrimony on bad investments.

derivedWords.definition: n. 源于父名的姓或名字 (e.g. Robinson)
derivedWords.example: Robinson is a patronymic meaning son of Robin.

derivedWords.definition: n. 父权；父系；父亲身份
derivedWords.example: A DNA test was ordered to establish paternity.

derivedWords.definition: adj. 父亲的；得自父亲的
derivedWords.example: My paternal grandfather was a doctor.

derivedWords.definition: n. 家长；族长；男统治者
derivedWords.example: The family patriarch gathered everyone for dinner.

derivedWords.definition: n. 杀父；杀父者；杀父罪
derivedWords.example: He was convicted of patricide after the investigation.
The matriarch ruled the village with wisdom.

derivedWords.definition: n. 母性，母道 adj. 产科的
derivedWords.example: She is currently on maternity leave.

derivedWords.definition: n. 主妇；保姆; 稳重妇女
derivedWords.example: The strict school matron kept order in the dorms.
She dressed in a quiet, matronly fashion.
Harvard is my alma mater.

derivedWords.definition: n. 结婚，婚礼；婚姻生活
derivedWords.example: They joined in holy matrimony.
The tragic news was reported as a suicide.

derivedWords.definition: n. 妇女联谊会；女学生联谊会
derivedWords.example: She joined a college sorority during her freshman year.

derivedWords.definition: n. 杀害亲姐妹者或行为
derivedWords.example: The play is a dark tragedy about sororicide.
Soldiers were forbidden to fraternize with the enemy.
They shared a deep, fraternal bond.

derivedWords.definition: n. 杀害兄弟者或行为
derivedWords.example: The civil war was a scene of fratricide.
The police treated the case as a homicide.
She had a regal bearing that demanded respect.
The crown is the ultimate symbol of regality.
The king dressed in full royal regalia.

derivedWords.definition: n. 弑君；弑君者；弑君罪
derivedWords.example: The assassination of King Charles was a famous regicide.

derivedWords.definition: adj. 溺爱妻子的；怕老婆的
derivedWords.example: He is an uxorious husband who agrees to every wish.
They resolved their marital differences through counseling.
He confessed to having an extramarital affair.
They signed a premarital agreement.
The United Nations condemned the genocide.
Nero was accused of matricide.
He was sent to prison for parricide.
We sprayed insecticide to protect the crops.
Huge is a synonym for large.
Cold is the antonym of hot.
The word 'bark' (tree covering vs. dog sound) is a homonym.
To, too, and two are homophones.

mainWords.meaning: 沙文主义者，盲目爱国者 / blind patriot

roots.name: chauvinist
roots.meaning: Nicolas Chauvin (blindly loyal soldier of Napoleon)

derivedWords.definition: n. 沙文主义者，盲目爱国者
derivedWords.example: The politician was a chauvinist who looked down on other nations.

mainWords.meaning: 偏执狂 / single-minded fanatic

derivedWords.example: Judaism is a classic example of monotheism.
His obsession with cleanliness bordered on monomania.
The inventor was a monomaniac when it came to perpetual motion.
His dipsomania led him to spend all his earnings at the pub.
The wealthy woman had a psychological condition of kleptomania.
The arsonist was diagnosed with pyromania.
The dictator's megalomania was evident in his giant statues.
The medical study discussed the history of nymphomaniac cases.
He sought therapy for his satyromania.
He felt a wave of claustrophobia inside the tiny elevator.

derivedWords.definition: n. 旷野恐怖症；广场恐怖
derivedWords.example: Her agoraphobia made her afraid to leave her house.

derivedWords.definition: n. 恐高症，高处恐怖症
derivedWords.example: His acrophobia made hiking in the high mountains impossible.
The acrophobe refused to look down from the balcony.

mainWords.meaning: 打破旧习者 / challenger of tradition

derivedWords.definition: n. 偶像破坏者；提倡打破旧习的人
derivedWords.example: The artist was an iconoclast who challenged mainstream trends.

derivedWords.definition: n. 打破旧习；破坏偶像主义
derivedWords.example: The scientific revolution was a time of great iconoclasm.

mainWords.meaning: 无神论者 / non-believer in God

derivedWords.example: His philosophy was rooted in secular atheism.
An agnostic believes the existence of God is unknown.
The doctor confirmed the diagnosis after several tests.
She is a skilled diagnostician of rare diseases.
The post-surgery prognosis was excellent.
Ancient Greek religion was based on polytheism.
Spinoza was a philosopher famous for his pantheism.
He spent years studying theology at the seminary.

mainWords.meaning: 色狼 / lustful man, libertine

derivedWords.example: The old lecher kept making inappropriate comments.
He was famous for his life of drinking and lechery.

derivedWords.definition: adj. 性欲 of lust;好色的
derivedWords.example: The novel was criticized for its libidinous themes.

derivedWords.definition: adj. 好色的；淫荡的；挑动情欲的
derivedWords.example: She gave him a lascivious wink.
His lubricious behavior made everyone uncomfortable.
He was arrested for making lewd gestures in public.
She tried to ignore his lustful stares.
The tabloids feed the prurient interest of the public.

mainWords.meaning: 臆想狂 / person obsessed with imaginary illness

derivedWords.example: His hypochondria made him visit the hospital weekly.

derivedWords.definition: n. 臆想狂；过分忧虑健康者
derivedWords.example: The hypochondriac was convinced a sneeze meant a serious disease.
Severe hypotension can cause dizziness and fainting.
Eating too much salt can lead to hypertension.

chapterTitle: How To Talk About a Variety of Personal Characteristics

mainWords.meaning: 巴结的，奉承迎合 / subservient, fawning

derivedWords.definition: adj. 巴结的，谄媚的，奉承迎合的
derivedWords.example: The waiters were obsequious, bowing low at every order.
The family gathered for the late king's obsequies.
Subsequent events proved that his theory was correct.
The movie was so popular that they filmed a sequel.
The cards were arranged in numerical sequence.

mainWords.meaning: 抱怨的 / complaining, whining

derivedWords.definition: adj. 抱怨的，爱发牢骚的
derivedWords.example: The querulous customer complained about the service.

mainWords.meaning: 傲慢的 / arrogant, condescending

derivedWords.definition: adj. 傲慢的；目空一切的
derivedWords.example: She gave them a supercilious look and walked away.

mainWords.meaning: 任性的，喧闹的 / noisy, unruly, hard to control

derivedWords.definition: adj. 任性的；喧闹的；难控制的
derivedWords.example: The obstreperous children refused to go to sleep.

mainWords.meaning: 一贫如洗 / penniless, poor

derivedWords.definition: adj. 一贫如洗的；贫困的
derivedWords.example: The impecunious student lived on noodles.
He had no pecuniary interest in the business.

mainWords.meaning: 有骑士风度的 / courteous, gallant

derivedWords.definition: adj. 彬彬有礼的；有骑士风度的
derivedWords.example: He made a chivalrous gesture by offering his coat.
The royal cavalcade moved through the city.

derivedWords.definition: adj. 傲慢的 n. 骑士
derivedWords.example: She dismissed the complaints in a cavalier manner.
The cavalry charged across the plain.
The age of chivalry is long gone.

derivedWords.definition: n. 骑手 adj. 骑马的
derivedWords.example: She won gold in the equestrian event.
The skilled equestrienne completed the jump easily.
The vet specialized in equine diseases.

mainWords.meaning: 无害的 / harmless, safe

derivedWords.definition: adj. 无害的；无伤大雅的
derivedWords.example: The joke was innocuous and offended no one.
The jury found the defendant innocent.
The factory emitted noxious fumes.

mainWords.meaning: 喝酒的，饮酒的 / fond of drinking

derivedWords.definition: adj. 喝酒的；吸水性强的
derivedWords.example: The bibulous uncle was always near the bar.
Plants imbibe moisture from the soil.
The baby wore a clean bib for dinner.

mainWords.meaning: 形容枯槁 / pale, looking like a corpse

derivedWords.definition: adj. 形容枯槁的；像尸体一样的
derivedWords.example: He had a cadaverous face after weeks of illness.
Medical students dissect a cadaver in anatomy class.
The decadent lifestyle led to their ruin.

mainWords.meaning: 痛苦的，悲哀的 / sorrowful, painful

derivedWords.example: The dolorous song brought tears to her eyes.
She expressed her deep dolor at the loss.
He gave me a doleful look when I said goodbye.
We condole with you on your great loss.

chapterTitle: How To Talk About Liars And Lying

mainWords.meaning: 臭名昭著的骗子 / widely unfavorably known

derivedWords.definition: adj. 臭名昭著的，声名狼藉的
derivedWords.example: He is a notorious liar whose words cannot be trusted.
The gangster gained notoriety for his crimes.

mainWords.meaning: 说谎老手 / perfect, highly skilled

derivedWords.definition: adj. 完美的；圆满的 vt. 完成；实现
derivedWords.example: He is a consummate artist who spends hours practicing.

mainWords.meaning: 积习难改的骗子 / beyond reform, uncorrectable

derivedWords.definition: adj. 积习难改的；不可救药的
derivedWords.example: The incorrigible child refused to sit still.

mainWords.meaning: 说谎成瘾的人 / chronic, long-established

derivedWords.definition: adj. 根深蒂固的；成瘾的
derivedWords.example: An inveterate gambler has a hard time quitting.

derivedWords.definition: n. 根深蒂固，积习，慢性
derivedWords.example: The inveteracy of his habits made change impossible.

mainWords.meaning: 天生的骗子 / existing from birth

derivedWords.example: He has a congenital heart defect.
The scientist did research in molecular genetics.
Genes determine physical traits.
The genealogist traced our family tree.
The genesis of the project was a simple idea.

mainWords.meaning: 常年说谎的骗子 / repeating, constant

derivedWords.definition: adj. 长期的；慢性的；习惯性的
derivedWords.example: He suffers from chronic back pain.

derivedWords.definition: n. 时代错误，不合潮流的人/物
derivedWords.example: A typewriter is an anachronism in a modern office.
The photos were arranged in chronological order.
The history teacher asked for a chronology of the war.
Ships use a chronometer for navigation.
The chronologist verified the timeline of the ancient ruins.

derivedWords.definition: v. 使同步；使同时发生
derivedWords.example: Let's synchronize our watches before starting.
We send our deepest sympathy to the family.
He studied pathology at the medical center.
He made no attempt to hide his antipathy to the program.
Public apathy led to low voter turnout.
She had deep empathy for the homeless.
The lost puppy was a pathetic sight.
They communicated by telepathy.

mainWords.meaning: 病态撒谎者 / diseased liar

derivedWords.definition: adj. 病态的；由疾病引起的
derivedWords.example: She is a pathological spender who is deep in debt.

mainWords.meaning: 无意的说谎者 / without conscience

derivedWords.definition: adj. 无良心的；不合理的；过分的
derivedWords.example: Taking all the profit for yourself is unconscionable.

derivedWords.definition: n. 不受良心控制；违背良心
derivedWords.example: The contract was void due to its unconscionability.

derivedWords.definition: adj. 全知的；无所不知的
derivedWords.example: The storyteller seemed to have an omniscient view.

derivedWords.definition: adj. 预知的；有先见之明的
derivedWords.example: Her prescient warning saved them from disaster.
The professor tolerated errors but not absolute nescience.

mainWords.meaning: 油嘴滑舌的骗子 / smooth, slippery tongue

derivedWords.definition: adj. 油嘴滑舌的；油腔滑调的
derivedWords.example: He gave a glib excuse that fooled no one.

mainWords.meaning: 恶名昭彰的骗子 / outstandingly bad

derivedWords.definition: adj. 恶名昭彰的；极坏的；过分的
derivedWords.example: It was an egregious error that cost them the match.

derivedWords.definition: vi. 聚集 vt. 聚集
derivedWords.example: People began to congregate in the town square.

derivedWords.definition: n. 分离，隔离；种族隔离
derivedWords.example: Civil rights leaders fought to end segregation.

derivedWords.definition: v. 聚集；总计 adj. 聚合的
derivedWords.example: The scores aggregate to a new record.

chapterTitle: How To Talk About Various Speech Habits

mainWords.meaning: 沉默寡言的 / silent, quiet

derivedWords.definition: adj. 沉默寡言的；不太说话的
derivedWords.example: He is a taciturn man who rarely speaks first.
Her taciturnity made her seem unfriendly.

derivedWords.definition: adj. 缄默的；不言而喻的；心照不宣的
derivedWords.example: They had a tacit agreement not to talk about it.

mainWords.meaning: 简洁的，简明的 / brief, pithy

derivedWords.example: The loquacious guide spoke non-stop for hours.
Hamlet's famous soliloquy begins with 'To be or not to be'.
She sat by the window, soliloquizing about her future.
The ventriloquist made the dummy sing.
He used colloquial expressions in his speech.

derivedWords.definition: n. 婉转的说法；累赘的陈述
derivedWords.example: Write directly and avoid circumlocution.
She gave an eloquent plea for justice.
The politician used magniloquent words to sound smart.
The commander gave a laconic reply: 'No'.
His speech was marked by extreme laconism.
The fish has a bright orange ventral fin.
Blood is pumped out of the left ventricle.
Ventricular contraction pumps blood throughout the body.
The shark's dorsal fin cut through the water.
The senator decided to endorse the bill.
The auricle helps collect sound waves.

derivedWords.definition: adj. 心胸宽广的；大度的
derivedWords.example: He was magnanimous in defeat, congratulating his opponent.
The media magnate owned several television networks.
This novel is considered the writer's magnum opus.
They ordered a magnum of champagne for the celebration.
The view from the top is magnificent.
We did not realize the magnitude of the problem.
His grandiloquent style was outdated.

mainWords.meaning: 口齿不清的 / speech-impaired, silent

derivedWords.definition: adj. 口齿不清的；说不出话的
derivedWords.example: He was inarticulate with rage.
She wrote an article about language study.

mainWords.meaning: 唠叨的，多嘴的 / chatterbox, talkative

derivedWords.definition: adj. 唠叨的；喋喋不休的
derivedWords.example: The garrulous old man told stories all afternoon.
Her garrulity drove her friends away.

mainWords.meaning: 陈腐的，平庸的 / trite, unoriginal

derivedWords.definition: adj. 陈腐的；平庸的；老一套的
derivedWords.example: The movie script was filled with banal dialogue.

mainWords.meaning: 冗长的，啰嗦的 / wordy, repetitive

derivedWords.example: The report was verbose and needed editing.
His verbosity made his speech boring.
She copied the letter verbatim.
They reached a verbal agreement.

derivedWords.definition: n. 冗词；废话；措辞方式
derivedWords.example: The document was full of legal verbiage.

mainWords.meaning: 健谈的 / talkative, fluent

derivedWords.example: The voluble hostess welcomed the guests warmly.
The earth revolves around the sun.
The industrial revolution changed society.
Do not involve me in your argument.
The business evolved from a small shop.
Her volubility helped her win the debate.

mainWords.meaning: 强有力的，令人信服的 / convincing, compelling

derivedWords.definition: adj. 强有力的；使人信服的
derivedWords.example: The lawyer made a cogent argument in court.
The cogency of her reasoning convinced us.

mainWords.meaning: 大声喊叫的 / loud, noisy, clamorous

derivedWords.definition: adj. 大声叫的；喊叫的；喧嚷的
derivedWords.example: The vociferous crowd protested outside the hall.
The boring lecture had a somniferous effect.
Stress caused his chronic insomnia.
The warm room made him feel somnolent.
Somnambulism is rare but can be dangerous.
The patient was ambulatory after surgery.
She pushed the baby in a perambulator.
They ambled through the park on Sunday.
The constitution begins with a brief preamble.

derivedWords.definition: adj. 催眠的 n. 安眠药
derivedWords.example: The warm milk has a soporific effect.
